description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a coarse, granular texture and a mottled pattern of light brown, tan, and gray hues. The surface is uneven, with visible indentations and a natural, weathered appearance, suggesting exposure to the elements over time. One side of the stone features a distinct dark marking resembling the letter 'K', adding a unique identifying feature. The stone sits atop a smooth, cylindrical black pedestal, which contrasts sharply with the roughness of the stone. The overall form is rounded but asymmetrical, with a noticeable groove running along one axis. The stone's coloration and texture are typical of granite or similar igneous rocks commonly found in urban landscaping or as natural barriers.
description: A rough, mottled granite stone with an irregular, rounded shape and a distinct dark marking, resting on a smooth black pedestal.
description_view_0: The stone appears elongated with a prominent groove and a coarse, speckled surface.
description_view_1: A rounded face of the stone is visible, featuring a clear dark 'K' marking.
description_view_2: The stone's broadest side is shown, highlighting the deep central groove and rough texture.
description_view_3: The stone looks more spherical from this angle, with mottled coloring and no visible markings.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Mottled speckling with a single dark marking resembling a letter; irregular and natural, not geometric.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with visible pitting and surface irregularities indicating prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smooth, man-made surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements.
